summary.what_happened: Checkout errors rose from 09:00; the session cache hit rate collapsed while the payment gateway stayed at baseline.
summary.customer_impact: A few percent of checkouts fail on first attempt.

incident_window.rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-12T09:14:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; deviated series not recovered -> ongoing

severity.explanation: checkout error rate 3.8% against a 0.4% baseline, inside the 1-25% SEV-2 band on a revenue-critical flow

timeline.description: WARN: evictions spiking: 1200 keys/s under memory pressure (maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru)
ERROR: session lookup miss for cart 88231; recomputing cart state from origin
ERROR: session lookup miss for cart 90417; recomputing cart state from origin
ERROR: timeout rebuilding cart from cold state after 1500ms; returning 500
ERROR: timeout rebuilding cart from cold state after 1500ms; returning 500
Alert fired: Checkout error rate above 1% on checkout-service
WARN: evictions spiking: 1400 keys/s under memory pressure (maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru)

evidence.interpretation: deploys.json is an empty list: the change window was checked and nothing shipped - internal change ruled out as trigger
session-cache eviction storm (1200-1400 keys/s) begins 08:59, checkout session misses and cold-rebuild timeouts follow
checkout-service error rate first crossed 2x baseline at 09:00 (1.8%)
payment-gateway p95 stayed at baseline (117-125ms) through the whole window - CONFLICTS with any gateway-outage explanation

hypotheses.title: session-cache eviction storm forcing cold cart rebuilds
hypotheses.statement: Cache evictions from 08:59 collapsed the hit rate; checkout rebuilt carts from cold state and timed out, consistent with the 500s. The flat payment-gateway series conflicts with any broader payment-path outage.
assumptions: cart rebuild is the dominant source of the 500s
cache memory pressure is not itself caused by a traffic surge
recommended_checks: inspect session-cache memory usage and key-size distribution
sample 500 responses to confirm cold-rebuild timeouts dominate

missing_data.impact: cannot identify slow spans or failing dependencies
no operational guidance or known failure modes available

recommended_next_steps.description: inspect session-cache memory usage and key-size distribution
sample 500 responses to confirm cold-rebuild timeouts dominate
Provide or repair the package data: traces.json not provided
Provide or repair the package data: runbook.md not provided

signals.recovered_when: within 10% of baseline 0.4 % for >= 3 consecutive points
within 10% of baseline 96 % for >= 3 consecutive points

log_patterns_should_stop: session lookup miss for cart N; recomputing cart state from origin
timeout rebuilding cart from cold state after Nms; returning N
recovery_verification.re_alert_condition: error_rate_pct on checkout-service crosses the alert threshold 1 again

checks.detail: SEV-2 is within the numeric ceiling (SEV-2)

communication_drafts.internal_update: SEV-2, ongoing: ~4% of checkouts fail. Leading hypothesis (medium confidence): session-cache eviction storm forcing cold cart rebuilds; the flat payment-gateway series conflicts with a wider payment outage, which caps confidence. No remediation has been executed; options await human approval.

postmortem_draft.title: Postmortem draft: checkout errors 2026-06-12
postmortem_draft.summary: Session-cache evictions collapsed the hit rate; checkout cold rebuilds timed out for a few percent of carts.
contributing_factors: likely: cache memory pressure and eviction storm
conflicting signal: payment gateway healthy throughout
open_questions: what filled the cache - key growth or memory shrink?
action_items: alert on eviction rate before hit-rate collapse
